text.title: Competitor Analysis Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Critical agent failure modes: hallucinated numbers, pricing-page footnotes, geography drift, context overflow on 10+ competitors.

antipattern.description: Accepting funding figures, employee counts, or revenue estimates without a verified source.
antipattern.reason: Without Crunchbase API access, agents invent plausible numbers. Mitigation: hard rule "if Crunchbase API unavailable, write 'unknown' — never estimate". Mark every unverified number with "(est.)".

antipattern.description: Accepting a pricing number without checking effective monthly cost for 1 user billed monthly.
antipattern.reason: Shown price excludes annual lock-in, seat minimums, and transaction fees. Always ask "what is the effective monthly price for 1 user, billed monthly?" before accepting any pricing cell.

antipattern.description: Trusting G2 or Capterra star ratings as objective quality signals.
antipattern.reason: Paid placement skews stars upward; incentivised reviews under-report weaknesses. Cross-check with Reddit and Hacker News sentiment before quoting any rating.

antipattern.description: Listing 5+ future competitors with no concrete signal (job posting, acquisition, beta product).
antipattern.reason: Agents over-list "Google could enter here" type future competitors. Cap at 2-3 and require one concrete signal per future competitor or it inflates the threat map.

rule.statement: Pin target geography in every prompt and reject any competitor whose primary market differs from your target region.
rule.rationale: Without geography pinning, agents return competitors ranked by Google's global SERP, which skews toward US companies even when the user's market is EU or APAC.

antipattern.description: Dumping raw scrapes from 10+ competitor websites into a single synthesis prompt.
antipattern.reason: Context overflows degrade gap analysis quality. Pre-summarise each competitor to the snapshot template (one Task call per competitor), then synthesise the summaries in a final pass.

rule.statement: Run per-competitor snapshot tasks in parallel, synthesis serially. Cuts wall-clock by 5-8x and isolates per-competitor context windows.
rule.rationale: Each competitor's pricing page, review scrape, and feature list is independent. Parallelising collection then serialising synthesis is the only pattern that scales beyond 5 competitors without context degradation.

list.item: Approve the seed competitor list before deep dives — agents over-index on Google's first page and miss "what my friend uses".
Review per-competitor snapshots for hallucinated facts before the gap analysis runs.
Sign off on the differentiation statement before it lands in marketing copy.
